summary: PR 2598 splits and renames the old Kirby Zelda/Sheik special-N translation-unit area: it introduces `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` as a Matching object, renames the remainder of `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` to `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, adjusts GALE01 text/sdata2 split ranges, and moves nearby functions/data out of `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, `ftKb_Init.c`, and `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c` to align ownership with the new files.

key_files.role: Updated split boundaries for Kirby special files: shortened `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, replaced `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` with `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, and assigned text/sdata2 ranges.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: config/GALE01/splits.txt hunk at Kirby special entries

key_files.role: Build configuration now lists `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` as Matching and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` as NonMatching instead of the old `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` object.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py MatchingFor object list hunk

key_files.role: New linked source file containing the Zelda-copy special-N functions from 0x80105B2C through 0x80105FEC, including motion-state entry, animation, physics, and collision handlers.
Renamed from `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c`; retains the remaining Sheik-copy special-N area after the new Zelda split and gains local ownership of constants formerly in `ftKb_Init.c`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: rename from `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` to `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, similarity index 90%
raw_diff: declarations/data added near top of `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`

key_files.role: Removed a Zelda special-N entry function and no-longer-needed externs after narrowing the Donkey split endpoint.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` removes `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C` and extern sdata2 floats

key_files.role: Removed declarations/data that were relocated to the renamed/split special files.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes `fn_801090D4` declaration, `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, and two Vec3 constants

key_files.role: Receives `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260`, which was removed from the renamed Sheik file.

what_changed: Split metadata changed from a single `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` range (`0x80105BA8`-`0x80109290`, sdata2 `0x804D94EC`-`0x804D9554`) to two files: `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` (`0x80105B2C`-`0x80105FEC`, sdata2 `0x804D94F0`-`0x804D94F8`) and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` (`0x80105FEC`-`0x80109290`, sdata2 `0x804D94F8`-`0x804D9554`).

`ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` was added with the Zelda special-N helper and handlers, including `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C`, `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105BA8`, `ftKb_ZdSpecialN_Anim`, `ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Anim`, IASA stubs, Phys handlers, and Coll handlers.
`ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` no longer carries `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C` or externs for `ftKb_Init_804D94F0` and `ftKb_Init_804D94F4`.
`ftkirbyspecialseak.c` is the renamed old `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` and loses the Zelda-special block plus `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260`.
`ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260` moved into `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c`, where its split now begins at `0x80109290` immediately after the Sheik file.
`ftKb_Init.c` stopped owning `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, `ftKb_Init_803B7570`, and `ftKb_Init_803B757C`; the diff shows these constants defined in `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` instead.

smart_moves.text: The PR updates `splits.txt` and `configure.py` in the same change as the source-file rename/split, keeping object naming, ranges, and build object status aligned.
The new matching object is limited to a narrow Zelda range rather than claiming the entire former `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` region.
Function/data movement follows split-boundary ownership: the function at `0x80109260` moves to `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c`, and the old `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` remainder is renamed to a Kirby special Sheik file.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a contiguous old TU actually covers multiple character-copy special ranges, use split boundaries and object configuration to separate the matching portion from the remaining nonmatching portion instead of treating the old filename as authoritative.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` replaced by `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` in splits/configure

decomp_lessons.lesson: Relocating functions across adjacent Kirby special files may also require relocating nearby constants/declarations so the owning TU matches the updated split ranges.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: constants removed from `ftKb_Init.c` and defined in `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`

naming_conventions.convention: Kirby copied-special source files in this area use lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c` filenames, as shown by replacing `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` with `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` next to existing `ftkirbyspecialfox.c`, `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` entries.
This PR does not rename the internal recovered function symbols away from address-style or existing `ftKb_*` names; filename ownership changes are stronger evidence here than semantic symbol renames.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` still contains names such as `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C` and `ftKb_ZdSpecialN_Anim`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use split-file boundaries to make only the newly isolated `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` object Matching while leaving the larger renamed `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` object NonMatching.

The new Zelda file uses literal `0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f` arguments in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` where the removed Donkey-hosted version referenced sdata2 externs `ftKb_Init_804D94F0` and `ftKb_Init_804D94F4`; the split also assigns `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` its own sdata2 range.

Some matching-steering artifacts remain in the moved code, including `PAD_STACK`, a `volatile unsigned int pad`, and comments such as `Permuter slop`; the PR evidence does not include verifier logs explaining these tactics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` contains `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(20)`, `volatile unsigned int pad`, and `Permuter slop` comments

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for cleanup of `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` PAD_STACK or `Permuter slop` comments.
Search for later renames of internal `ftKb_SpecialNZd_*` symbols after the `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` file split.
Search current splits/path facts for whether `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` spelling is retained as canonical.
Search later PRs touching `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, `ftKb_Init_803B7570`, or `ftKb_Init_803B757C` to confirm final data ownership.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR 2598 metadata and changed-file counts
raw_diff: full diff for 7 changed files
notes: Diff evidence is strong for file moves, split ranges, object status, and exact source edits.

There is no PR body, review discussion, or validation log in the loaded slice, so matching rationale and verifier evidence cannot be independently confirmed beyond `configure.py` marking `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` as Matching.
No reviewer feedback is available in the slice.
The PR title supports a link/rename interpretation, but does not explain why `seak` is the chosen spelling or whether internal function names were intentionally left unchanged.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split old mixed Kirby special TUs by address ownership before marking a subsection Matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2598 shows a focused way to link a contiguous Kirby special subsection: update `splits.txt`, add the new object in `configure.py` as Matching, keep the remaining renamed file NonMatching, and move adjacent functions/data to the owning split instead of over-claiming the old TU.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: source movement across Kirby special files

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby Zelda/Sheik special split introduced by PR 2598
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` was replaced in this PR by `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` for text `0x80105B2C`-`0x80105FEC` and `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialseak.c` for text `0x80105FEC`-`0x80109290`; configure marks the Zelda file Matching and the Sheik file NonMatching in the PR diff.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path/split history for future curator lookup; should be verified against current source before promotion because this intake only records the historical PR slice.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting split-first linking for mixed legacy TUs
source_update_candidates.text: When linking a small matched subsection from a legacy mixed TU, update split metadata and build-object status together, keep unmatched remainder separate, and move data/functions to the owning split range. PR 2598 is supporting evidence but not enough by itself for a broad global rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` marked Matching while `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` remains NonMatching
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standard or lesson candidate, but curator should compare with other PRs before elevating it beyond this PR-specific pattern.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all remaining `PAD_STACK`, volatile locals, or `Permuter slop` patterns in the new file are accepted style; this slice has no objdiff/regression evidence explaining them.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale from the merge; there are no loaded reviews or comments.
Do not promote `seak` spelling as a global naming rule without checking current source/path facts; the PR only proves this historical file path in the diff.
Do not claim a verified code match from tooling logs; the only loaded evidence is the `configure.py` Matching entry and the merged PR metadata.
